preamble: Velar palatalization of the voiced velar stop [g] to the voiced postalveolar affricate [dʒ] is a parallel process to [k] > [tʃ], often occurring before front vowels or glides. This shift, often occurring via a palatal stage [ɟ], is central to the Slavic First Palatalization, the development of voiced affricates in Old English, and the Romance languages (e.g., Latin 'gentem' > Italian 'gente' [dʒ]).

examples.note: Development before front vowels. E.g., Russian 'živ' (alive).
Vocative form showing palatalization of /g/ before front vowel /e/.
Geminate velar /gg/ palatalized and affricated to [dʒ].

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Schenker, A. M. (1995). The Dawn of Slavic.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
